title: NotebookLM is now bigger than Gemini
summary: Jeff Su reports that NotebookLM now has higher search interest than Google Gemini. He highlights NotebookLM's core advantage as providing grounded answers from multiple document formats rather than hallucinations, suitable for accuracy-critical tasks with known sources.
writeup: Notebook LM has surpassed Google Gemini in search interest, and this short explainer argues that its core advantage remains unchanged: grounded answers pulled from documents the user already holds. A concrete health insurance scenario (PDF brochure, spreadsheet, webinar recording) illustrates how Notebook LM synthesizes across incompatible formats into a single cited answer. The through-line is a three-condition decision rule that identifies exactly when Notebook LM will outperform every other AI tool.

key_points: Notebook LM now has more search interest than Google Gemini, despite both receiving frequent updates.

The health insurance example is the clearest illustration of its niche: a user combines a PDF, a spreadsheet, and a webinar recording and asks which provider offers the best dental coverage, receiving a grounded answer without manually reviewing each source.

The three-condition rule for choosing Notebook LM: the user already knows which documents contain the answers, those sources come in different formats or mediums with no single source giving the full picture, and the stakes are too high to tolerate hallucinations.
When all three conditions hold, Notebook LM is argued to outperform every other AI tool available.

chapters.title: Notebook LM Context and Core Advantage
